package_description: Operational freight-efficiency package covering telematics, eco-driving, maintenance discipline, idle reduction, and related management improvements on base diesel freight.
rollout_limit_notes: Operational freight packages can scale faster than vehicle turnover, but keep uptake below full diesel activity because persistence depends on management quality and the role remains too coarse for duty-cycle-specific assumptions.
evidence_summary: Australian freight guidance supports a narrow low-cost operational package on the incumbent diesel method that stays materially smaller than the existing efficient-diesel pathway method.
derivation_method: Use a conservative fuel-only multiplier and a small output-cost adder so the package remains an operational wedge rather than silently recreating the efficient-diesel pathway.
review_notes: The package applies only to the diesel incumbent; efficient diesel, BEV, and FCEV remain excluded to avoid double counting route-specific improvements.
